identity.short_name: Pell (D) -> pick p\equiv5 and q\equiv1 from x1^2+1 -> inert/split counts -> lift to p^a,q^b -> CRT count mod 1000

problem_spec.summary: Compute the fundamental Pell solution, select two primes from x1^2+1 via distinct mod-8 filters, force opposite local splitting types by reciprocity, count solutions to x^2-Dy^2\equiv1 modulo a coprime product of two prime powers, and report the count mod 1000.
givens: $D>1$ is squarefree and not a perfect square.
$D$ has at least two distinct odd prime factors.
$(x_1,y_1)$ is the fundamental positive solution to $x^2-Dy^2=1$ (minimal $x_1>1$), and $y_1>1$.
There exists an odd prime divisor of $x_1^2+1$ congruent to $5\pmod 8$; let $p$ be the smallest such.
There exists an odd prime divisor of $x_1^2+1$ congruent to $1\pmod 8$; let $q$ be the smallest such.
ask: Let $C$ be the number of ordered pairs $(x,y)\in(\mathbb{Z}/N\mathbb{Z})^2$ such that $x^2-Dy^2\equiv1\pmod N$. Determine the unique $A\in\{0,1,\dots,999\}$ satisfying $A\equiv C\pmod{1000}$.

parameter(s).name: D
parameter(s).domain: squarefree nonsquare integer $D>1$ with at least two distinct odd prime factors and $y_1>1$, such that $x_1^2+1$ has at least one prime divisor $\equiv5\pmod8$ and at least one prime divisor $\equiv1\pmod8$
parameter(s).purpose: sets the Pell discriminant and guarantees two deterministically selected primes with opposite local splitting behavior

parameter(s).name: a
parameter(s).purpose: exponent of the prime power modulus built from the inert prime $p\equiv5\pmod8$

parameter(s).name: b
parameter(s).purpose: exponent of the prime power modulus built from the split prime $q\equiv1\pmod8$

invariant(s).name: minimal_x_normalization
invariant(s).statement: Among positive integer solutions to $x^2-Dy^2=1$ with $x>1$, there is a unique one with minimal $x$; this defines $(x_1,y_1)$.

invariant(s).name: dual_residue_class_prime_selection
invariant(s).statement: Define $p$ as the smallest prime divisor of $x_1^2+1$ with $p\equiv5\pmod8$ and $q$ as the smallest prime divisor of $x_1^2+1$ with $q\equiv1\pmod8$; when they exist, $(p,q)$ is uniquely determined.

invariant(s).name: Pell_to_QR_bridge
invariant(s).statement: For any odd prime $\ell\mid(x_1^2+1)$ one has $x_1^2\equiv-1\pmod\ell$ and hence from $x_1^2-Dy_1^2=1$ obtains $Dy_1^2\equiv-2\pmod\ell$, so $\left(\frac{D}{\ell}\right)=\left(\frac{-2}{\ell}\right)$.

invariant(s).name: split_inert_dichotomy_from_mod8
invariant(s).statement: The residue class of $\ell\bmod8$ fixes $\left(\frac{-2}{\ell}\right)$; thus $\ell\equiv5\pmod8$ forces $\left(\frac{D}{\ell}\right)=-1$ (inert) while $\ell\equiv1\pmod8$ forces $\left(\frac{D}{\ell}\right)=1$ (split).

invariant(s).name: uniform_prime_power_lift_growth
invariant(s).statement: For any odd prime $r\nmid D$, each solution of $x^2-Dy^2\equiv1\pmod r$ lifts to exactly $r^{t-1}$ solutions modulo $r^t$, so the count modulo $r^t$ equals $r^{t-1}$ times the count modulo $r$.

invariant(s).name: CRT_multiplicativity_of_solution_counts
invariant(s).statement: For coprime moduli $m,n$, the solution set modulo $mn$ to a single congruence is in bijection with the product of solution sets modulo $m$ and modulo $n$; thus counts multiply for $N=p^a q^b$.

essential_assumption(s): The condition $y_1>1$ excludes degenerate Pell cases where the fundamental solution is immediate.
Existence of both congruence classes among prime divisors of $x_1^2+1$ is required to define $(p,q)$ and the product modulus $N=p^a q^b$.
The exponents satisfy $a\ge2$ and $b\ge2$ so prime-power lifting is genuinely required in both local factors.
uniqueness_certificate(s): The fundamental solution $(x_1,y_1)$ is unique by minimal-$x$ normalization. The primes $p$ and $q$ are uniquely determined as the smallest prime divisors of $x_1^2+1$ in the congruence classes $5\pmod8$ and $1\pmod8$, respectively. The Legendre-symbol bridge and reciprocity force the local inert/split regimes, determining the local solution counts modulo $p$ and $q$; multivariate Hensel-style lifting fixes counts modulo $p^a$ and $q^b$; CRT multiplicativity fixes the global count modulo $N$. Therefore $C\bmod1000$ (and hence $A$) is unique.

technique(s).name: continued_fraction_optional
technique(s).purpose: compute the fundamental solution $(x_1,y_1)$ via the periodic continued fraction of $\sqrt D$

technique(s).name: residue_class_filtered_prime_selection
technique(s).purpose: deterministically select $p$ and $q$ from the prime divisors of $x_1^2+1$ using the congruence filters $\equiv5\pmod8$ and $\equiv1\pmod8$
outputs: the primes $p,q$ and the modulus $N=p^a q^b$

technique(s).name: Pell_to_character_bridge
outputs: a forced local character identity at selected primes

technique(s).name: quadratic_reciprocity_evaluation
technique(s).purpose: evaluate $\left(\frac{-2}{\ell}\right)$ using supplementary laws and force split/inert behavior

technique(s).name: inert_vs_split_local_counting
technique(s).purpose: count solutions modulo $r$ to $x^2-Dy^2\equiv1$ by norm-kernel size (inert) or factorization to $uv=1$ (split)

technique(s).name: prime_power_solution_count_via_lifting
technique(s).purpose: lift solutions uniformly from modulo $r$ to modulo $r^t$ for $r=p,q$

technique(s).name: Chinese_remainder_multiplicativity
technique(s).purpose: combine local counts to obtain the global count modulo $N=p^a q^b$

theorem(s).name: Pell_equation
theorem(s).role: provides the continued-fraction characterization of the fundamental solution and its uniqueness

theorem(s).name: Euler_criterion_and_Legendre_symbol
theorem(s).role: justifies converting quadratic congruences into Legendre symbol identities in the Pell-to-character bridge

theorem(s).name: quadratic_reciprocity
theorem(s).role: evaluates $\left(\frac{-2}{\ell}\right)$ from $\ell\bmod8$ and forces the inert/split dichotomy

theorem(s).name: Finite_field_multiplicative_group_cyclic
theorem(s).role: ensures $\mathbb{F}_{r^2}^\times$ is cyclic, enabling the norm-kernel count $r+1$ in the inert case

theorem(s).name: Hensel_lifting_for_multivariate_congruences
theorem(s).role: guarantees uniform $r$-to-1 lifting per level for nonsingular solutions, giving the $r^{t-1}$ growth

theorem(s).name: Chinese_remainder_theorem
theorem(s).role: gives the bijection between solutions modulo $p^a q^b$ and pairs of solutions modulo $p^a$ and modulo $q^b$

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Norms in quadratic extensions plus CRT decomposition: the congruence $x^2-Dy^2\equiv1$ is a norm-$1$ condition; inert primes use a quadratic field extension, split primes factor to $uv=1$, and CRT multiplies the local counts.

critical_trick(s): Use two mod-8 residue-class filters on prime divisors of $x_1^2+1$ to force one inert local regime and one split local regime via the Pell-to-character bridge and quadratic reciprocity.
Count solutions locally (inert: norm-1 kernel size $p+1$; split: factorization gives $q-1$), lift to prime powers, then multiply via CRT for $N=p^a q^b$ and reduce modulo $1000$.

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: continued fractions, Pell verification, factorization, Legendre symbols, and symbolic canonicalization for the novelty audit

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: integer square roots for a bounded Pell preflight scan and modular arithmetic scaffolding

library(s).name: random
library(s).reason: randomized sampling of solutions to test lift behavior and CRT recombination checks

library(s).name: itertools
library(s).reason: deterministic enumeration of a small set of symmetry transforms in the novelty equivalence audit

import_hint(s): from sympy import factorint, legendre_symbol, continued_fraction_periodic

task(s).name: primary_structural_check
task(s).reason:
Correctness checks consistent with the intended path, including required preflight attempts:
Attempt A (naive-guess mismatch): pretend both selected primes behave as inert and replace the split count (q-1) by (q+1); this produces residue 436 mod 1000, which differs from the true residue 832.
Attempt B (bounded search fails): brute-scan for Pell solutions x^2-Dy^2=1 with 1<=y<=1259 and find none; the first solution occurs at y=1260.
Attempt C (partial info non-unique): ignore the Pell-driven prime selection and vary primes only under the relaxed conditions p\equiv5 (mod 8), (D/p)=-1 and q\equiv1 (mod 8), (D/q)=1; among primes <200 this yields many distinct residues mod 1000 (24 distinct values).
Main structural validations:
- computed the periodic continued fraction of sqrt(D) and confirmed the fundamental solution (x1,y1).
- factorized x1^2+1 and applied the residue-class filters to select (p,q).
- verified (D/p)=-1 and (D/q)=1 via legendre_symbol / Euler criterion.
- brute-counted solutions modulo p and modulo q to confirm base counts p+1 and q-1.
- verified the lift lemma by counting lifts (a,b) for random solutions modulo r^t (r in {p,q}) for t=1,2.
- verified CRT recombination by combining random local solutions modulo p^a and q^b and checking the congruence modulo N.

task(s).name: secondary_independent_check
task(s).reason:
Independent derivation of the same count:
- "for split q: pick s with s^2\\equivD (mod q) and reduce to uv\\equiv1 (mod q^b), giving exactly phi(q^b)=q^{b-1}(q-1) solutions."
- "for inert p: work in the finite field F_{p^2} and compute the norm-1 kernel size p+1; then use the uniform lift growth to get p^{a-1}(p+1)."
- multiply local counts (coprime moduli) and reduce the result mod 1000.

task(s).name: novelty_equivalence_audit
task(s).reason:
Canonicalized parent vs daughter defining constraint expressions using sympy:
expand -> together -> factor -> simplify, then normalized by dividing out coefficient gcd and fixing polynomial leading-sign under lex order.
Represented the modulus structure symbolically (P^4 for the parent vs P^3*Q^2 with congruence tags P-5 and Q-1 for the daughter).
Tried deterministic symmetry transforms: (x,y) swap; sign flips x->-x, y->-y; and p<->q swap (P<->Q) where applicable.
No transform produced identical canonicalized constraint multisets, so the daughter is not equivalent to the parent under the allowed disguises.
